chief_complaint: A 75-year-old male patient presented with sudden onset of vertigo, nausea, and left-sided weakness after undergoing carotid artery stenting.
history: The patient had a history of hypertension, hyperlipidemia, and smoking. He underwent carotid artery stenting 2 days prior to presentation. He reported no recent trauma or infection.
exam: Vital signs: Blood pressure 160/90 mmHg, pulse 80 beats per minute. Physical examination revealed left-sided weakness, nystagmus, and ataxia.
labs: ECG: Normal. Bloods: Elevated troponin levels. Imaging: Thrombi were directly visualized in the basilar and vertebral arteries on black-blood MRI.
